hero_subtitle: SFTY organizes MAS-specific regulatory workflows by connecting provider outputs, preserving audit evidence, and automating reporting to support VASP compliance teams efficiently.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Reporting workflows slow under fragmented processes
problem_description: Compliance teams managing MAS reporting face delays and manual burdens due to disconnected system outputs and scattered evidence. This fragmentation complicates audit readiness and obscures clear linkage between regulatory changes, reviews, and reporting actions.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY connects MAS provider outputs, regulatory updates, case evidence and reviewer actions into a unified workflow that streamlines reporting preparation and strengthens audit trail for compliance teams.
workflow_title: From regulatory updates to MAS reporting
workflow_description: SFTY maps MAS regulatory changes to internal rules then routes provider checks into cases, decisions, evidence and structured MAS reporting workflows.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Not another compliance tool. The MAS reporting layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects fragmented MAS provider outputs into structured workflows, cases, evidence and reports to streamline jurisdiction-specific compliance and support audit-ready MAS reporting.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs with structured reporting, audit-ready evidence and controlled deployment to meet MAS compliance and enhance operational governance from day one.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support MAS reporting workflow management
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how MAS regulatory rules integrate into case workflows
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit-ready evidence is preserved for MAS reports
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe provider orchestration for MAS jurisdiction-specific compliance
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for MAS compliance teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY helps MAS-facing compliance teams structure onboarding, monitoring, case management and reporting workflows to meet jurisdiction-specific requirements efficiently within digital asset businesses.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY support MAS reporting workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY connects alerts, checks and Travel Rule data into connected MAS reporting cases, supporting draft reports, internal review and structured evidence packages for efficient submissions.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves decision logs, case histories and evidence packages with detailed audit trails to support regulatory reviews and compliance inspection readiness.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing provider stacks?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY operates above existing KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers, orchestrating their outputs into consistent MAS-specific workflows without replacing them.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace MAS compliance providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY complements MAS providers by turning their outputs into operational workflows, evidence and reports rather than replacing underlying compliance systems.
final_title: Structure MAS reporting workflows with SFTY
final_description: Review how SFTY connects MAS-specific provider outputs into audit-ready cases and streamlined reporting workflows for VASP compliance teams.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.

footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
